title: rosa communis subsp. alpestris
intro: Rosa communis subsp. alpestris, also known as the alpine rose, is a subspecies of the wild rose plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Rosa communis subsp. alpestris is believed to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to treat various health conditions.
